steps.title: Identify the isosceles triangle property
steps.content: Since $PQ = QR$, triangle $PQR$ is isosceles with the equal sides meeting at $Q$. In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite the equal sides are equal. The angle opposite $PQ$ is angle $R$ (at vertex $R$), and the angle opposite $QR$ is angle $P$ (at vertex $P$). Therefore, angle $P$ = angle $R$.

steps.title: Find x using the base angle equality
steps.content: Since angle $P$ = angle $R$ and angle $P = 24°$, we have angle $R = 24°$. The angle at $R$ is labeled as $x°$ in the diagram, so $x = 24$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
